executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 organizations compromised across all sectors in the past 48 hours. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (luxury retail), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware gang leads the campaign with 20+ victims, followed by safepay, play, and clop groups. This represents a coordinated, large-scale attack wave targeting Canadian critical infrastructure, healthcare, education, and public sector entities.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 20+ Canadian victims including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, A Lococo Wholesale, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre (public sector), Pre-Con Builders, Bergmanis Preyra (law firm), Questica, Eastern Townships School Board (education), MG CPA, Watermark Beach Resort, CST Coal, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Maheu&Maheu, Berts Electric, Capp Shupak
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - Double extortion ransomware with data leak site

items.name: safepay

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a massive ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations compromised across all provinces and sectors in the past 48 hours. Critical infrastructure is under siege: public health (Peterborough Public Health), education (Eastern Townships School Board, teccart.qc.ca, baisyaakov.ca), municipal governments (Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network) have all been breached. No CCCS advisories were identified in this feed, but the scale and coordination of attacks suggest a national-level threat requiring immediate federal response.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (luxury retail) compromised by coinbasecartel ransomware gang. Active Green + Ross (automotive retail/services) compromised by sinobi ransomware gang.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (payday loan/financial services) compromised by everest ransomware with database leaked. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by sinobi. Trucash (financial services) compromised by lynx. Multiple accounting/financial firms affected: Pewarchuk CPA (play), MG CPA (qilin), reidhurstnagy.com (incransom), Axion50plus (devman).

report_2w.executive_summary: Multiple Palo Alto Networks authentication bypass vulnerabilities are being actively exploited in the wild, with CISA adding them to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. A major supply chain attack compromised Red Hat's npm packages, distributing credential-stealing malware that affects thousands of developers. GitHub experienced a security incident impacting internal repositories, prompting an official Canadian government (CCCS) alert. Iranian threat actors are conducting destructive attacks in the Middle East, wiping IT and recovery systems. Meta's AI support bot was exploited to hijack Instagram accounts, highlighting new AI-powered attack vectors.

items.description: PAN-OS authentication bypass allowing VPN hijack
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: Linux Copy Fail privilege escalation on shared build hosts
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Zero-click RCE vulnerability in Windows Netlogon domain controllers
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Container escape vulnerability affecting Linux containers
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium
Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Path traversal vulnerability allowing arbitrary file access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Low, Availability: Low

items.name: TeamPCP (suspected Russian-affiliated APT)
items.targets: GitHub repositories (3,800+ infected), software supply chain
items.ttps: T1195.001 (Supply Chain Compromise), T1554 (Compromise Host Software Binary), credential theft via malicious VS Code extension

items.name: Miasma (supply chain actor)
items.targets: Red Hat npm packages, JavaScript developers
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1555 (Credentials from Password Stores), self-propagating worm distribution

items.name: Dragon Weave (China-aligned)
items.targets: Czech Republic, Taiwan
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1598 (Phishing for Information)

items.name: Ababil of Minab (Iran-linked)
items.targets: Four countries in Middle East
items.ttps: T1485 (Data Destruction), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), wiping IT systems, backups, and recovery infrastructure

items.name: DragonForce Ransomware
items.targets: Shoreline Sightseeing (Chicago), Panorama BPO
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), double extortion tactics

items.name: DriveSurge
items.targets: Thousands of compromised websites
items.ttps: T1189 (Drive-by Compromise), T1204.001 (User Execution: Malicious Link), ClickFix fake update attacks

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Charter Communications (4.9M accounts)
items.ttps: T1598.003 (Phishing: Spearphishing via Service), vishing attacks targeting Salesforce credentials

items.name: Grandoreiro Banking Trojan
items.targets: Banks in Spain and Latin America
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie), banking credential theft

items.name: Silent Ransom Group
items.targets: US organizations
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), physical social engineering with USB devices

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-013 regarding a security incident impacting GitHub internal repositories, indicating potential risk to Canadian government and private sector developers. Canada's government warned that data from connected vehicles has 'intelligence value' for foreign adversaries, highlighting nation-state interest in transportation infrastructure data. Bill C-22 lawful access legislation is under discussion in Canadian parliament.
